title: Why You See Faces in Toast
script_summary: Exploring the phenomenon of pareidolia, where our brains see faces in everyday objects.

segments.voiceover: Ever see a face in your toast? You're not alone.
This phenomenon is called pareidolia. Our brains are hardwired to recognize faces.
It's a survival mechanism! Spotting faces helps us connect socially and identify threats.
Research in cognitive psychology shows this happens across cultures and ages.
So next time you see a face in your toast, remember: it’s your brain’s way of connecting the dots.
Like this? Follow for more fascinating insights into the human mind!
